resources.title: AI Agent Studio Governance Notes

resources.title: Quarterly Release Impact Planner

paths.title: Functional Consultant Starter
paths.summary: Build vocabulary around enterprise structures, workforce deployment, security basics, and interview-ready explanations.

paths.title: Implementation Lead Readiness
paths.summary: Practice project framing: scope, design workshops, tenant readiness, migration, integrations, testing, and release sign-off.

paths.title: AI Agent Studio for HCM
paths.summary: Learn how to think about HCM agents through governance, approved knowledge, persona testing, and sensitive-data boundaries.

moduleGuides.title: Core HR implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Use Core HR to explain the enterprise model, worker lifecycle, workforce structures, and the decisions that affect every downstream HCM module.
implementation: Confirm enterprise, legal employer, business unit, department, location, job, position, and grade usage.
Document country rollout waves and which structures are global versus local.
Validate reference data sharing, approvals, security, conversion, and reporting needs before configuration.
interview: Explain enterprise structure with a business example instead of a definition-only answer.
Describe why legal employer and business unit decisions affect reporting, security, and integrations.
Mention testing across hire, transfer, termination, manager self-service, and HR specialist transactions.

moduleGuides.title: HCM security implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Security design should separate what a user can do from which workforce records the user can access.
implementation: Map personas such as employee, line manager, HR specialist, payroll user, recruiter, and administrator.
Design role provisioning and data access before user acceptance testing.
Prepare positive and negative test cases for sensitive worker and salary information.
interview: Differentiate function access and data access in plain language.
Use a manager example to explain why security testing must include record visibility.
Call out segregation of duties, audit readiness, and least-privilege access.

moduleGuides.title: HCM Data Loader implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Migration success depends on ownership, data quality, sequencing, validation, and reconciliation, not just loading files.
implementation: Assign owners for source extraction, transformation, validation, loading, and sign-off.
Sequence foundation objects before workers, assignments, compensation, and module-specific records.
Track load errors, correction rules, reconciliation counts, and cutover timing.
interview: Explain why configuration and migration teams must work together.
Discuss common risks: invalid references, duplicate workers, date-effective issues, and missing mandatory values.
Describe how you would prove conversion accuracy before go-live.

moduleGuides.title: HCM Extracts implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Extracts translate HCM data into outbound files or payloads for payroll, benefits, identity, reporting, or third-party systems.
implementation: Confirm the receiving system, population criteria, frequency, file format, and delivery method.
Design filters, effective dates, change detection, and reconciliation totals.
Test initial, incremental, exception, and no-change scenarios.
interview: Explain extract design as a business data contract.
Mention scheduling, delivery, error handling, and downstream reconciliation.
Use a payroll or identity feed example to show practical understanding.

moduleGuides.title: Payroll implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Payroll design requires careful discovery across calendars, elements, costing, balances, retro rules, integrations, and controls.
implementation: Capture pay groups, payroll calendars, element categories, costing segments, and statutory variation.
Plan parallel payroll testing with clear success criteria and exception handling.
Coordinate payroll with Core HR, absence, time, benefits, finance, and reporting owners.
interview: Show awareness that payroll is highly country-sensitive and control-heavy.
Explain why parallel testing and reconciliation are non-negotiable.
Discuss upstream dependency risks from HR, time, absence, and compensation.

moduleGuides.title: Absence Management implementation guide
moduleGuides.summary: Absence design connects policy rules with eligibility, accruals, approvals, employee experience, payroll, and reporting.
implementation: Gather policy rules for types, plans, eligibility, accruals, carryover, waiting periods, and approvals.
Test employee submission, manager approval, HR correction, payroll impact, and reporting scenarios.
Confirm country or legal employer variations before global template sign-off.
interview: Explain the difference between absence types, plans, eligibility, and accrual logic.
Use maternity, sick leave, or annual leave as practical examples.
Mention integration points with payroll, time, and workforce scheduling.

labs.title: Design a two-country enterprise structure
labs.scenario: A company is rolling out HCM in India and the United States with shared departments but country-specific legal employers.
tasks: List enterprise, legal employer, business unit, department, location, job, and position decisions.
Identify which decisions are global and which are country-specific.
Write three interview points explaining your design.

labs.title: Create persona-based security tests
labs.scenario: An HR specialist, manager, and employee need different access to worker and assignment data.
tasks: Create one positive and one negative test for each persona.
Separate function access tests from data visibility tests.
Write a short risk note for over-permissioned users.

labs.title: Plan a worker conversion cycle
labs.scenario: A client wants to load foundation data and active workers before UAT.
tasks: Define the load sequence and owner for each stage.
Prepare validation checks for counts, dates, departments, managers, and assignments.
Create an error triage plan for failed worker records.

labs.title: Govern an HR policy assistant
labs.scenario: The HR team wants an AI agent to answer leave-policy questions for employees and managers.
tasks: Define allowed and blocked question types.
List approved knowledge inputs and ownership.
Create refusal and escalation examples for sensitive requests.

labs.title: Build a quarterly release impact board
labs.scenario: Your tenant is receiving a quarterly update and leadership wants a short readiness view.
tasks: Group changes by enabled module and business impact.
Identify regression tests, owners, and communications.
Write a one-paragraph executive readiness summary in your own words.

practiceCards.question: How would you explain the difference between legal employer and business unit in Oracle HCM?
practiceCards.answer: A legal employer is tied to employment and statutory responsibilities. A business unit is usually about transaction processing, reference data, and operational grouping. In interviews, connect both to the actual enterprise model.

practiceCards.question: What should you validate before configuring workforce structures?
practiceCards.answer: Validate rollout countries, legal entities, business units, departments, reference data sharing, security owners, and downstream reporting or integration needs.

practiceCards.question: Why are function access and data access treated separately?
practiceCards.answer: Function access controls what a user can do. Data access controls which people, organizations, or records the user can see. A consultant should design and test both.

practiceCards.question: What makes an HCM Data Loader cycle successful?
practiceCards.answer: A successful cycle has clean source data, correct sequencing, clear ownership, repeatable validation, documented errors, reconciliation totals, and business sign-off.

practiceCards.question: What is a responsible first step before building an HCM AI agent?
practiceCards.answer: Define the business purpose, owner, allowed knowledge inputs, security boundaries, escalation path, and test personas before configuration starts.

practiceCards.question: How should a quarterly HCM release review be converted into action?
practiceCards.answer: Map changes to enabled modules, identify optional feature decisions and regression needs, assign owners, test critical transactions, and communicate user-facing changes.

checklist: Document enterprise and legal structure decisions
Validate HCM security roles and data access
Prepare migration assumptions and reconciliation rules
Map quarterly release items to regression tests
Keep public learning content original and link-free

posts.title: Module guide: planning an absence policy workshop
posts.summary: A good absence workshop turns policy language into eligibility rules, plan behavior, approval flows, payroll touchpoints, and testable employee-manager scenarios.
takeaways: Capture plan rules, eligibility, accruals, carryover, and country variation.
Test employee submission, manager approval, HR correction, and payroll impact.
Convert every policy exception into a named test scenario.

posts.title: Interview drill: enterprise, legal employer, business unit, and department
posts.summary: For a consultant interview, answer with the business model first: who employs people, where transactions are processed, how departments group workers, and how reporting needs are met.
takeaways: Enterprise is the top-level HCM structure, while legal employers represent employment and statutory obligations.
Business units often support transaction processing and reference data decisions.
Departments, jobs, positions, grades, and locations make the workforce model usable for HR operations.

posts.title: Implementation use case: global HR rollout design workshop
posts.summary: A multinational rollout should begin with country scope, legal employer design, shared reference data, security responsibilities, migration assumptions, and a cutover plan before detailed configuration starts.
takeaways: Create a decision log for enterprise structure assumptions.
Separate global standards from country-specific statutory requirements.
Validate security and data roles before opening configuration to a wider team.

posts.title: AI Agent Studio for HCM: what to govern before the first agent
posts.summary: Before building an HCM agent, define the agent owner, allowed knowledge inputs, data boundaries, approval workflow, test scenarios, and escalation path for sensitive employee questions.
takeaways: Keep HR policy and employee data access under formal ownership.
Test with real HCM roles such as employee, line manager, HR specialist, and administrator.
Do not use confidential client material or restricted support content in public examples.

posts.title: Release readiness: how to build an HCM impact plan
posts.summary: Review quarterly change themes by product family, identify enabled features, check optional items, and build a focused regression pack for transactions that matter to your organization.
takeaways: Start with modules in production, then review adjacent shared areas such as security and common user experience.
Convert update themes into test cases, owner names, and go/no-go checkpoints.
Write original internal summaries instead of copying release-note text.
